spells.name: Ectoplasmic Shaping
spells.dice: Presence + Occult + Death vs. Resolve + Gnosis or Resistance
spells.description: The mage reshapes an existing mass of ectoplasm with his will, contesting control with whichever mage or ghost currently commands it.

spells.name: Forensic Gaze
spells.description: The mage stares at a corpse to learn what killed it and how long ago it died, no autopsy required.

spells.name: Grim Sight
spells.description: The mage activates Mage Sight through the lens of Death, perceiving the weight of mortality on people, places, and things and gaining a bonus to detect vampiric Disciplines, Devotions, and rituals.

spells.name: Shadow Sculpting
spells.description: The mage shapes existing shadows into forms of his choosing or thickens darkness to conceal objects and impede the vision of those who look into the area.

spells.name: Soul Marks
spells.description: The mage senses the spiritual health of a soul, discerning derangements, vampiric vessel status, or the telltale signs of a mage who has created a soul stone.

spells.name: Speak with the Dead
spells.description: The mage can see, hear, and speak with ghosts in Twilight, even detecting those who have hidden themselves or chosen not to reveal their presence.

spells.name: Animate Shadows
spells.description: The mage causes existing darkness to move of its own accord, directing it to flow from its natural resting place to wherever he desires, even beneath bright light.

spells.name: Corpse Mask
spells.description: The mage alters the apparent cause of death on a corpse, adding or removing wound features and disease signs to mislead forensic investigators.

spells.name: Decay
spells.description: The mage causes a material object to corrode, rust, or rot on command, reducing its Durability by one point per success in a way that cannot be repaired by mundane means.

spells.name: Ectoplasm
spells.description: The mage extrudes ectoplasm from one of his orifices, creating the opaque white substance that can be molded freely and used as a mirror for observers to see ghosts or objects in Twilight.

spells.name: Entropic Guard
spells.description: The mage converts incoming attack energy into primal decay, gaining one point of magical armor per dot he possesses in Death; spending one Mana extends the Duration to one day.

spells.name: Ghost Summoning
spells.description: The mage calls a specific ghost by name or broadcasts a general summons to all shades in sensory range, compelling the nearest or named ghost to come to him as quickly as it can travel.

spells.name: Grant the Grim Sight
spells.description: The mage bestows his Grim Sight upon another being, allowing even a supernatural creature or Sleeper to perceive the supernatural weight of Death for the duration.

spells.name: Soul Jar
spells.description: The mage enchants any sealable container to trap a soul within it, preventing the soul from escaping back into the cycle of birth and death.

spells.name: Suppress Aura
spells.description: The mage causes his own or another's aura to lapse into invisibility, imposing a cumulative penalty on any attempt to scrutinize the target's supernatural presence.

spells.name: Touch of the Grave
spells.description: The mage gains the power to physically interact with ghosts and ghostly objects in Twilight, touching and being touched by shades and their associated ephemeral anchors.

spells.name: Control Ghost
spells.description: The mage forces a ghost to obey simple verbal commands, issuing directives that the shade must work to fulfill for the duration of the spell.

spells.name: Destroy Ephemera
spells.description: The mage mystically attacks an ephemeral object, inflicting aggravated damage on its Structure for every success beyond the object's Durability.

spells.name: Destroy Object
spells.description: The mage mystically attacks a material object, causing it to instantly corrode and decay, inflicting aggravated damage on its Structure.

spells.name: Devouring the Slain
spells.description: The mage harvests Willpower or Mana from a dying or recently wounded person, drawing the energies that depart along with their fading vitality.

spells.name: Entropic Shroud
spells.description: The mage casts the Entropic Guard spell on others rather than himself, protecting a target with a field of decay that erodes the force of incoming attacks.

spells.name: Ghost Gate
spells.description: The mage creates a portal between the material world and Twilight, transforming anyone who passes through into a Twilight being for the duration of the scene.

spells.name: Ghostly Object
spells.description: The mage destroys a material object and casts its ghost into Twilight, creating a persistent spectral duplicate that can be manipulated with Death 2 magic.

spells.name: Quicken Corpse
spells.description: The mage animates a corpse as a tireless zombie servant that feels no pain, follows commands with mechanical devotion, and can be made Indestructible with enough successes.

spells.name: Restore Corpus
spells.description: The mage heals a ghost's ephemeral body, restoring one point of Corpus damage per success.

spells.name: Sculpt Ephemera
spells.description: The mage reshapes or reinforces objects and places within Twilight or the Shadow Realm, altering Durability or transforming raw ephemera into useful forms such as weapons or armor.

spells.name: Sever the Sleeping Soul
spells.description: The mage forcibly severs a Sleeper's soul from the body, leaving the victim suffering the full afflictions of soullessness for as long as the separation lasts.

spells.name: Summon Shadows
spells.description: The mage conjures darkness from nothing, creating a one-yard patch or five-cubic-yard volume of shadow that can serve as cover against ranged attacks and form a weak barrier.

spells.name: Suppress Own Life
spells.description: The mage temporarily suppresses his own vital signs and consciousness, appearing entirely dead with the onset of rigor mortis, until a pre-set trigger awakens him.

spells.name: Enervation
spells.description: The mage draws vigor from a target's body, reducing the victim's Strength Attribute by one dot per success down to a minimum of 1.

spells.name: Haunting
spells.dice: Presence + Persuasion + Death vs. Resolve or Resistance
spells.description: The mage compels a recently dead or dying Sleeper's spirit to cling to a physical anchor rather than moving on, forcing the shade to haunt that locale.

spells.name: Revenant
spells.description: The mage forces a recently dead person's soul back into an undecaying body, creating a driven revenant animated by assigned Passions and a pool of Essence.

spells.name: Rotting Flesh
spells.description: The mage riddles a living target's body with corruption and putrefaction, inflicting lethal damage per success and imposing Social roll penalties from the grotesque wounds.

spells.name: Slay Own Aura
spells.description: The mage permanently destroys all signature of his current magical aura, making it completely unreadable by any magic; a new aura forms naturally over the following week.

spells.name: Soul Binding
spells.description: The mage attaches a stolen soul to himself or another, placing the free soul in permanent residence within a host body that currently lacks one.

spells.name: Suppress Other's Life
spells.dice: Manipulation + Subterfuge + Death vs. Composure + Gnosis
spells.description: The mage suppresses another person's life signs entirely, making the target appear dead with all physical symptoms of death while leaving them in a deep, dreamless sleep.

spells.name: Twilight Shift
spells.description: The mage transforms himself into a Twilight being without passing through a gateway, adopting an ephemeral state that allows direct interaction with ghosts and ghostly objects.

spells.name: Devouring the Living
spells.action: Instant; subtract target's Stamina (living being) or Resistance (ghost)
spells.dice: Manipulation + Intimidation + Death – Stamina or Resistance
spells.description: The mage harvests Mana or Willpower from a healthy living person or nearby ghost, consuming spiritual energies from targets not already touched by the grave.

spells.name: Destroy Mana
spells.description: The mage destroys any form of stored Mana within sensory range, shattering magical reserves held in artifacts, soul stones, or another mage's personal pattern.

spells.name: Quell the Spark
spells.description: The mage dispels any active spell regardless of which Arcana created it, as long as he has Mage Sight active to perceive the target effect.

spells.name: Quicken Ghost
spells.description: The mage restores a mindless shade to a semblance of its former personality and raises each of its Attributes by one, potentially transforming ancient ghosts into formidable specters.

spells.name: Steal Lifespan
spells.description: The mage severs a portion of a Sleeper's remaining lifespan and weaves it into his own thread of existence, permanently extending his life at the victim's expense.

spells.name: Summon the Dead
spells.description: The mage calls a long-departed ghost from the Underworld, compelling the shade to appear in Twilight and answer one truthful question per success before departing.

spells.name: Influence Heat
spells.description: The mage guides the direction and flow of existing heat, redirecting warmth from one place to another without increasing its temperature or creating heat where none exists.

spells.name: Influence Light
spells.description: The mage guides the direction and flow of existing light, bending a beam to focus more tightly, splitting visible white light into its spectrum, or redirecting illumination without creating new light.

spells.name: Influence Sound
spells.description: The mage guides the direction and flow of existing sound, focusing whispers across a vast chamber or ensuring his voice carries only to the person he is addressing without amplifying its volume.

spells.name: Nightsight
spells.description: The mage attunes her perceptions to trace amounts of ambient light, heat, and vibration, allowing her to see in the dark with the same clarity as most nocturnal predators, though sudden violent motions may impose adjustment penalties.

spells.name: Read Matrices
spells.description: The mage activates Forces Mage Sight, perceiving the full spectrum of energies including heat, gravity, electromagnetism, radiation, and kinetic force as well as their magical resonance.

spells.name: Receiver
spells.description: The mage can hear sounds on subsonic or supersonic frequencies normally beyond human perception and can eavesdrop on electromagnetic data transmissions such as radio, cellular signals, and wireless communications, though she cannot transmit.

spells.name: Tune In
spells.description: The mage listens in on a free-floating data transmission such as that of a cellular modem and translates the electromagnetic noise into intelligible information, though she is restricted to eavesdropping on existing signals and cannot transmit.

spells.name: Control Heat
spells.description: The mage can increase or decrease the temperature of an area, raising or lowering it by up to 10 degrees Fahrenheit per success within a one-yard radius.

spells.name: Control Light
spells.description: The mage can brighten or weaken an existing light source, with each success doubling or halving the light's output; at this level the mage cannot create light where none exists.

spells.name: Control Sound
spells.description: The mage can amplify or turn down the volume of sound in an area, with each success doubling or halving a sound source emanating from within a targeted one-yard-radius spot.

spells.name: Eyes of the Matrix
spells.description: As the Forces 1 Read Matrices spell, except the mage can cast this on a non-mage or even a supernatural being; if cast on a Sleeper, Disbelief is invoked immediately unless the Duration is less than one scene.

spells.name: Influence Electricity
spells.action: Instant and aimed; subtract target's Defense
spells.description: The mage influences the direction or flow of an existing electrical current, causing it to flash or arc toward a target of his choice; he cannot amplify the current or generate new electricity at this level.

spells.name: Influence Fire
spells.action: Instant and aimed; subtract target's Defense
spells.description: The mage influences the direction or flow of an existing flame, directing it toward a target or redirecting its course without making it larger or hotter at this level.

spells.name: Invisible Object
spells.description: The mage renders an inanimate object, including cameras, invisible to all forms of sight; if the object is moved it becomes visible while in motion, and sounds it makes can be redirected in combination with Influence Sound.

spells.name: Kinetic Blow
spells.description: The mage focuses the force of a physical attack into a pinpoint, converting bashing damage to lethal as though the person on the other end were stabbed with a knife.

spells.name: Transmission
spells.description: The mage can hijack an extant radio signal, transmitting her own words on it and replacing the original transmission, though mimicking a specific voice or talent requires additional mundane Skills.

spells.name: Unseen Shield
spells.description: The mage deflects incoming attacks with an invisible energy shield, gaining one point of magical armor per dot in the Forces Arcanum; spending Mana can extend the Duration to one day.

spells.name: Autonomous Servant
spells.description: The mage directs a portion of his own mind to guide a telekinetic force through an extended physical task — cooking, picking a lock, lifting buckets — while he tends to other mental activity. Requires Mind 1 or 5; Space 2 allows the servant to act out of the caster's line of sight.

spells.name: Bestow Unseen Shield
spells.description: The mage casts the Forces 2 Unseen Shield spell upon others rather than himself, protecting allies with a field of deflected kinetic force.

spells.name: Call Lightning
spells.description: The mage calls down lightning from a pre-existing or mage-created storm to strike a target of his choice, inflicting three points of bashing damage plus one per success; he cannot conjure lightning from nothing.

spells.name: Control Electricity
spells.description: The mage can diminish an electrical current or alter its direction of flow, redirecting power between outlets or cutting it selectively; he cannot generate new electricity at this level.

spells.name: Control Fire
spells.description: The mage can fuel an existing fire to increase its size and intensity, or deprive it of fuel to dampen or extinguish it entirely.

spells.name: Light Mastery
spells.description: The mage can create or extinguish light, conjuring illumination ranging from a flashlight to stadium intensity from thin air, or plunging a lit area into complete darkness.

spells.name: Personal Invisibility
spells.description: The mage turns herself invisible, requiring Concentration to remain unseen; quick movements cause a visible blur, and she cannot cast other spells or make attacks without revealing her location.

spells.name: Sound Mastery
spells.description: The mage creates sounds from nothing or silences existing sounds, producing anything from a whisper to industrial noise, and can record sounds magically as stored bits of energy for later playback.

spells.name: Telekinesis
spells.description: The mage can telekinetically lift and move objects remotely, declaring before casting whether successes apply to Strength for lifting or Dexterity for fine manipulation; the object cannot be moved beyond the mage's sight.

spells.name: Telekinetic Strike
spells.description: The mage creates a ball of telekinetic force and hurls it at an intended target, inflicting one point of bashing damage per success.

spells.name: Turn Projectile
spells.description: The mage can direct the course of a fast-moving projectile, turning it up, down, left, right, or back the way it came; once altered, the projectile continues on its new trajectory until something else changes it.

spells.name: Bestow Invisibility
spells.description: The mage turns another person invisible, as with the Forces 3 Personal Invisibility spell, though the target must maintain Concentration to remain unseen.

spells.name: Burst of Speed
spells.description: The mage reduces forward friction from the atmosphere and vastly amplifies momentum, increasing his Speed by his full Speed rating again for each success; a Mana point can be spent to boost Speed for an additional turn.

spells.name: Change Weather
spells.description: The mage alters weather patterns in an area, bringing almost any sort of normal non-disastrous weather to bear given sufficient time and effort; major events such as hurricanes or tornadoes require the Forces 5 Adverse Weather spell.

spells.name: Control Velocity
spells.description: The mage can increase or decrease the velocity of an object, doubling or halving the target's Speed per success; the mage can even halt high-speed projectiles, although living creatures resist with a reflexive Composure + Gnosis roll.

spells.name: Fiery Transformation
spells.description: The mage transforms into a living being of flame, dealing lethal fire damage to anyone who makes physical contact while retaining her Physical Attributes; requires Life 4, and Matter 4 can transform worn equipment to fire as well.

spells.name: Friction Knife
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Stamina + Gnosis reflexively
spells.dice: Intelligence + Occult or Science + Forces vs. Stamina + Gnosis
spells.description: The mage increases the friction between a subject and the surrounding air to a lethal degree, inflicting one Health wound of lethal damage per every three yards the target moves in a turn.

spells.name: Levitation
spells.description: The mage lifts himself off the ground using telekinetic force and travels in any direction at a maximum Speed equal to his Gnosis +1 per success, though he must maintain Concentration as an instant action each turn.

spells.name: Thunderbolt
spells.description: The mage conjures crackling tendrils of electricity at her fingertips and sends them at a ranged target, inflicting one Health wound of lethal damage per success; spending one Mana makes the damage aggravated.

spells.name: Transform Energy
spells.description: The mage transforms one form of energy into another — sound into light, electricity into heat, fire into electricity — without increasing or diminishing its overall potency.

spells.name: Unseen Hand
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Composure + Gnosis reflexively
spells.dice: Wits + Brawl + Forces vs. Composure + Gnosis
spells.description: The mage telekinetically lifts a living creature into the air, contesting the target's Composure + Gnosis to establish the grip; the mage can move the creature at a Speed equal to his Gnosis plus any excess successes.

spells.name: Adverse Weather
spells.description: The mage creates a major weather disturbance such as a tsunami, monsoon, or tornado on a catastrophic scale, following the same guidelines as the Forces 4 Change Weather spell but capable of genuinely destructive phenomena.

spells.name: Bestow Burst of Speed
spells.description: The mage grants others the vastly accelerated movement he can bestow upon himself with the Forces 4 Burst of Speed spell; the target, not the mage, may spend Mana to boost Speed for additional turns.

spells.name: Bestow Levitation
spells.description: The mage imparts levitation to others as he does for himself with the Forces 4 Levitation spell, though only willing targets can receive the effect and the target must maintain Concentration to remain aloft.

spells.name: Complete Invisibility
spells.description: The mage turns herself invisible without needing Concentration to remain unseen, gaining her Defense against attacks and the ability to move freely, though quick movements still cause a faint blur visible to attentive observers.

spells.name: Control Gravity
spells.description: The mage redirects the force of gravity, causing people and things to fall in another direction such as sideways or upward; a simple success affects a one-yard radius, with excess successes spreading the effect over a larger area.

spells.name: Create Sunlight
spells.description: The mage creates a miniature sun, generating a source of true sunlight that is especially deadly to vampires within the illuminated radius, with the area of direct sunlight scaling with successes.

spells.name: Earthquake
spells.description: The mage creates a localized earthquake by manipulating the balance of force and pressure beneath the ground, inflicting damage on structures within the targeted area and requiring Dexterity + Athletics from living beings to remain standing.

spells.name: Electromagnetic Pulse
spells.description: The mage creates an EMP that destroys power and electrical devices within the spell's radius, ruining the electronic components of everything affected; the pulse radius expands with additional successes.

spells.name: Eradicate Radiation
spells.description: The mage extinguishes a source of nuclear radiation in an area, permanently neutralizing the ongoing hazard with a lasting effect that scales in radius with additional successes.

spells.name: Flight
spells.description: The mage acquires the power of true flight, repelling herself from the Earth's surface and gaining full maneuverability without needing Concentration to stay aloft, though she gains her Defense only against attacks that can reach her.

spells.name: Increase Gravity
spells.description: The mage increases the pull of gravity in an area as if the Earth were a larger body, reducing Speed and imposing Physical dice penalties on those within the volume when the gravity's Strength exceeds their own.

spells.name: Nullify Gravity
spells.description: The mage decreases the pull of gravity within a five-cubic-yard volume as if the Earth were a smaller body, allowing anyone within to move and jump as though in low gravity with dramatically increased vertical range.

spells.name: Radiation
spells.description: The mage irradiates an area, suffusing it with deadly radiation that inflicts escalating effects on living beings over time from weakness through nausea and bleeding to eventual fainting and death.

spells.name: Velocity Mastery
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Composure + Gnosis reflexively
spells.description: The mage can completely control an object or living creature's velocity, speeding it up or slowing it down beyond personal control and even halting bullets in mid-air; the effect lasts for the object's Duration or one turn against living creatures.

spells.name: Analyze Life
spells.dice: Intelligence + Survival (plants) or Animal Ken (animals) or Medicine (humans) + Life
spells.description: The mage discerns the species, age, and sex of a nearby living plant, animal, or human, and can identify breeds or detect whether a non-human supernatural creature belongs to a known species.

spells.name: Cleanse the Body
spells.description: The mage cleanses herself of drugs, toxins, or poisons, adding spellcasting successes as bonus dice to overcome a substance or ignoring an equal number of points of poison damage.

spells.name: Healer's Trance
spells.description: The mage discerns the state of health of a living being and identifies any illnesses or injuries; obscure or rare diseases may impose a dice penalty to the roll.

spells.name: Pulse of the Living World
spells.description: The mage gains the Mage Sight and detects the presence of mystic vital animation in the area, reading the resonance of living energies as a function of reality's living forces.

spells.name: Sense Life
spells.description: The mage senses the proximity of living creatures within her sensory range and creates a safety zone around herself into which no living thing can enter without her immediate awareness.

spells.name: Body Control
spells.description: The mage gains perfect conscious control of his bodily functions — breathing, reflexes, metabolism, and circulation — allowing him to slow his heart, halve healing time, and regulate biological needs with a single spell.

spells.name: Control Base Life
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Stamina reflexively
spells.dice: Manipulation + Animal Ken + Life vs. Stamina
spells.description: The mage makes a base life form engage in any behavior natural for it, triggering instinctual responses such as feeding, fleeing, or secreting venom through manipulation of brain and glandular chemistry.

spells.name: Heal Flora and Fauna
spells.description: The mage heals the wounds of animals and plants; the mage must touch the target at this level.

spells.name: Organic Resilience
spells.description: The mage shores up her living Pattern with magic, gaining one point of magical armor per dot she possesses in the Life Arcanum; spending one Mana extends the Duration to one day.

spells.name: Purify Bodies
spells.description: The mage cleanses others of drugs, toxins, or poisons, functioning as the Life 1 Cleanse the Body spell cast upon other targets.

spells.name: Self-Healing
spells.description: The mage heals herself of injuries and wounds, restoring one point of bashing or lethal Health damage per success starting from the rightmost wound on the Health track.

spells.name: Self-Purging
spells.description: The mage cures a sickness or disease in herself; the rarity and virulence of the disease determines the target number of successes required, with Ebola requiring ten or more.

spells.name: Transfer Base Features
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Stamina reflexively
spells.dice: Intelligence + Animal Ken or Survival + Life vs. Stamina
spells.description: The mage gives one base life form features from another base life form, transferring one feature per success — such as an earthworm with chitinous armor or a dragonfly with photosynthetic skin.

spells.name: Transform Base Life
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Stamina reflexively
spells.dice: Intelligence + Animal Ken or Survival + Life vs. Stamina
spells.description: The mage transforms one base life form such as insects, fungi, or plants into another base life form entirely, or converts a plant's living energy into an insect swarm.

spells.name: Visions of the Living World
spells.dice: Intelligence + Animal Ken or Survival + Life
spells.description: As the Life 1 Pulse of the Living World spell, but the mage casts this on another mage or supernatural being to bestow simple Life Arcanum perceptions upon them.

spells.name: Banish Plague
spells.description: The mage cures sickness or disease in others, functioning as the Life 2 Self-Purging spell cast upon other targets.

spells.name: Control Median Life
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Resolve reflexively
spells.dice: Presence + Animal Ken + Life vs. Resolve
spells.description: The mage exerts telepathic control over a median life form such as a mammal, bird, or reptile, forming a bond that compels the animal to react in virtually any desired way within its normal behavioral range.

spells.name: Degrading the Form
spells.description: The mage lowers one Physical Attribute of herself or a base or median life form by one dot per success, causing the target to appear or function as infirm, feeble, or impaired.

spells.name: Healing Heart
spells.description: The mage heals the wounds of other humans, restoring one bashing or lethal Health wound per success.

spells.name: Honing the Form
spells.description: The mage raises one of his Physical Attributes or those of a base or median life form by one dot per success, up to a maximum equal to his Life Arcanum dots.

spells.name: Organic Shield
spells.description: As the Life 2 Organic Resilience spell, but the mage can now cast this magical armor upon other people rather than only herself.

spells.name: Transfer Median Features
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Stamina reflexively
spells.description: The mage gives a median life form features from another median or base life form, transferring one feature per success — such as crocodilian limbs on a shark or a scorpion's stinger tail on a spider monkey.

spells.name: Transform Median Life
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Stamina reflexively
spells.description: The mage transforms one form of median life into another median or base life form, converting mammals, fish, birds, or reptiles into different species of comparable or smaller size.

spells.name: Transform Self
spells.description: The mage gives himself the features of a base or median life form, developing fungal, plant, or animal traits to adapt to a given environment or set of circumstances.

spells.name: Two Faces
spells.description: The mage can alter his own superficial physical features, changing one feature per success such as eye color, skin color, hair, facial structure, and other distinguishing marks.

spells.name: Animal Degradation
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Stamina reflexively
spells.dice: Presence + Animal Ken + Life vs. Stamina
spells.description: The mage degrades more than one Physical Attribute of a base or median life form simultaneously, allocating the spell's successes among the target's Physical Attributes in any combination.

spells.name: Animal Minion
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Resolve reflexively
spells.description: The mage exerts gross bodily control over a base or median life form using the affected creature like a marionette, pushing it beyond its natural instinctual parameters through delicate manipulation of brain chemistry.

spells.name: Contagion
spells.description: The mage causes sickness or disease in a target, mimicking an existing contagious illness that anyone exposed to the infected target may catch with a reflexive Stamina + Athletics or Survival roll.

spells.name: Doppelganger
spells.description: The mage alters her build and physical features to conform precisely to those of another human being, effectively becoming that person in appearance regardless of gender, age, or size differences.

spells.name: Enfeeblement
spells.description: The mage degrades one of another person's Physical Attributes, lowering it by one dot per success up to a maximum equal to his Life Arcanum dots.

spells.name: Hone Another's Form
spells.description: The mage raises one of another human being's Physical Attributes as per the Life 3 Honing the Form spell applied to others rather than the caster.

spells.name: Life Force Assault
spells.description: The mage attacks the mystical life force generated by all living things, inflicting one point of lethal damage per success directly upon a single living target.

spells.name: Many Faces
spells.description: The mage changes another human being's features as per the Life 3 Two Faces spell, altering the target's physical appearance one feature per success.

spells.name: Shapechanging
spells.description: The mage takes on the physical characteristics — including Physical Attributes and Size — of an average member of any biological species, from ant to elephant.

spells.name: Supreme Honing
spells.description: The mage can raise more than one of his Physical Attributes simultaneously, dividing spellcasting successes among different Attributes as preferred; each Attribute is still limited by his Life dots.

spells.name: Transform Other
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Stamina + Gnosis reflexively
spells.dice: Presence + Animal Ken + Life vs. Stamina + Gnosis
spells.description: The mage gives another human being features from a base or median life form, as per the Life 3 Transform Self spell applied to others at this level.

spells.name: Trigger the Lizard Brain
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Resolve + Gnosis reflexively
spells.dice: Manipulation + Intimidation + Life vs. Resolve + Gnosis
spells.description: The mage controls the instinctual reactions of human beings as he does with lower life forms, triggering hardwired biological responses such as fear, submission, or territorial aggression through glandular chemistry.

spells.name: Uplift the Species
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Stamina reflexively
spells.description: The mage can change a form of base life into a form of median life, elevating insects, fungi, or other base organisms into non-sentient mammals, birds, or reptiles.

spells.name: Create Life
spells.description: The mage can create a living biological creature from nothing, up to the complexity of any natural organism; without Mind Arcanum, the construct is mindless and motivated only by instinct.

spells.name: Evolutionary Shift
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Stamina reflexively
spells.description: The mage transforms a base or median life form into a human being, granting it human physical characteristics while leaving its mind with only the basic instinctual programming of the creature it was.

spells.name: Fantasia
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Stamina reflexively
spells.dice: Stamina + Animal Ken + Life vs. Stamina + Gnosis
spells.description: The mage performs the most fantastic alterations to extant living creatures, adding or subtracting any ability that could conceivably belong to a natural life form — from venom sacs to biochemical flame production.

spells.name: Greater Shapechanging
spells.action: Instant or contested; target rolls Stamina + Gnosis reflexively
spells.description: The mage can change shape without the risk of being lost in her new form and can change others with equal facility, retaining full reason during any transformation she or her targets undergo.

spells.name: Hereditary Change
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Stamina reflexively
spells.description: Cast in combination with a spell that alters a creature's features, this spell makes those changes hereditary and permanent, effectively altering the subject's genome so that offspring inherit the new traits.

spells.name: Human Minion
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Resolve + Gnosis reflexively
spells.dice: Manipulation + Persuasion + Life vs. Resolve + Gnosis
spells.description: The mage exerts gross bodily control over human beings as per Animal Minion, but humans cannot be compelled to endanger themselves or act radically against their beliefs without a fresh contested resistance roll.

spells.name: Regeneration
spells.description: The mage temporarily regenerates his own or another person's lost limb or organ, with the number of successes needed scaling from one for a finger or toe up to eight for the brain.

spells.name: Ultimate Degradation
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Resolve + Gnosis reflexively
spells.dice: Strength + Athletics + Life vs. Resolve + Gnosis
spells.description: The mage can lower more than one of a person's Physical Attributes simultaneously, distributing the spell's successes to devastate a target's physical capabilities across multiple areas.

spells.name: Ultimate Honing
spells.description: The mage raises more than one of another human being's Physical Attributes simultaneously, as per the Life 4 Hone Another's Form spell distributed across multiple Attributes.

spells.name: Interconnections
spells.description: The mage reads the hidden threads of destiny linking people, places, and things, discerning the strength of sympathetic connections and detecting supernatural manipulations of fate. With enough successes she can also expose indirect supernatural mind control, spiritual possession, or alterations of someone's destiny.

spells.name: Quantum Flux
spells.description: The mage reads probability and compensates for small deleterious factors in the immediate environment, spending turns aiming to negate up to three dice worth of penalties from minor negative circumstances on a one-for-one basis. Successive uses toward the same goal are considered Vulgar.

spells.name: Reading the Outmost Eddies
spells.description: The mage knows the outcome of some small random action in her immediate vicinity — which scratch ticket to buy or which machine will drop a stuck item — though the spell cannot produce life-altering changes or alter events already in motion.

spells.name: Sharpshooter's Eye
spells.description: The mage eliminates all minor random factors working against a ranged attack, granting a flawless lock on a target and reducing penalties from range, position, size, and environment by one die per success. Requires Space 1 and either Life 1 for living targets or Matter 1 for objects.

spells.name: The Sybil's Sight
spells.description: The mage activates Fate Mage Sight, perceiving the weight of fortune on people and things and sensing the particular qualities a being's destiny gives it in Fate-related interactions. While this spell is in effect, a reflexive Wits + Investigation roll is made to sense when something of metaphysical weight has been spoken or done nearby.

spells.name: Winds of Chance
spells.description: The mage evades or attracts small turns of good or ill fortune, nudging quantum probabilities so that minor lucky or unlucky coincidences befall the target — enough to make someone smile or to ruin their day, but incapable of producing life-altering changes.

spells.name: Exceptional Luck
spells.description: The mage's endeavors are blessed with exceptional luck that defies the odds; she gains the 9-again quality on one future dice roll per success, with the player declaring which rolls are affected before those dice fall. The effect lasts one scene unless additional Duration factors are added during casting.

spells.name: The Evil Eye
spells.description: The mage directs a hex of immediate ill-fortune at a target, subtracting one die per Fate dot she possesses from the victim's very next dice pool, with each extra success on the spellcasting roll extending the curse to one additional roll. The spell can be cast as a prepared conditional spell to delay the onset of the curse.

spells.name: Fortune's Protection
spells.description: The mage weaves probability around herself so that attacks just happen to miss and knives slide off her jacket at the last moment, gaining one point of magical armor per dot she possesses in Fate. Spending one Mana extends the Duration to one day.

spells.name: Granting the Sybil's Sight
spells.description: The mage bestows Fate Mage Sight upon another being, allowing even a Sleeper or supernatural creature to perceive the interplay of destiny for the duration. If cast upon an unwilling target, he may contest the spell with a reflexive Resolve + Gnosis roll; if cast on a Sleeper, it invokes Disbelief immediately unless the Duration is less than one scene.

spells.name: The Perfect Moment
spells.description: The mage acts in a social situation with perfect Fate-guided grace and timing, saying exactly the right thing at exactly the right moment without any foreknowledge of what he will encounter. Casting this spell repeatedly on the same subject tangles their fates and may lead to unfavorable complications.

spells.name: Platonic Mechanism
spells.description: The mage imbues a mechanical or electronic device with perfect precision, causing it to function exactly as designed for the duration free from jams, drift, or inexplicable quirks, as though it had achieved its Platonic ideal form. The device is no more resilient to damage than normal, but it performs at 100% physical efficiency.

spells.name: Shifting the Odds
spells.description: The mage nudges destiny to generate a run of good or bad luck through completely plausible circumstances, steering fortune in the desired direction via tiny cascading causes. Successive attempts toward the same goal are considered Vulgar.

spells.name: Swearing an Oath
spells.description: The mage formally declares her intentions to Creation, placing the full metaphysical weight of destiny behind a vow so that honoring it allows a reflexive Resolve + Composure roll to act despite opposing compulsions. Breaking the oath knowingly afflicts the caster with the Evil Eye until the spell is dispelled.

spells.name: Alter Oath
spells.description: The mage rewrites the terms and conditions of an existing Fate-based oath, changing what the target must do to honor or violate it; altering another mage's oath without her knowledge can itself be treated as intending to violate that vow. A mage cannot alter her own oath unless she has Fate 4.

spells.name: Bestow Exceptional Luck
spells.description: Like the Fate 2 Exceptional Luck spell, the mage grants the 9-again quality to another person for one or more future dice rolls, bestowing a gift of fortune that can prove lifesaving. All orders recognize the value of this rote for protecting allies.

spells.name: Fabricate Fortune
spells.description: The mage creates a false destiny upon a person or thing, making it seem favored or cursed by fate to all those sensitive to fortune and prophecy. Those attuned to destiny must overcome the spell's Potency to see through the deception; if cast upon an unwilling mage, a reflexive contested Composure + Gnosis roll may be made.

spells.name: Grant Fortune's Protection
spells.cost: 1 Mana (optional to extend Duration to 24 hours)
spells.description: Like the Fate 2 Fortune's Protection spell, the mage weaves a shield of probability around another person rather than herself, causing attacks to improbably miss through bad timing and poor luck. Spending one Mana can extend the Duration to 24 hours.

spells.name: Lucky Coin
spells.description: The mage brings good fortune to a lifeless object, imbuing it with destined luck so it receives an equipment dice bonus equal to the spell's successes (up to the caster's Fate dots) and may have its armor value increased by one per success. Lucky objects tend to benefit those who own them even without being actively used.

spells.name: Monkey's Paw
spells.description: The mage brings bad fortune to a lifeless object, cursing it so it imposes an equipment dice penalty equal to the spell's successes (up to the caster's Fate dots) and can reduce a suit of armor's protection by one per success, even down to zero. Unlucky objects tend to cause harm to their owners even without being actively employed.

spells.name: Occlude Destiny
spells.description: The mage shields a destiny from outside perception or magical alteration, protecting a true child of prophecy from detection or guarding an oath from having its conditions perceived or changed by another willworker's magic. The spell's Potency contends against all other mages' attempts to perceive or alter the target's destiny.

spells.name: Superlative Luck
spells.description: The mage's endeavors are blessed with amazing, credibility-defying luck; she gains the 8-again quality on one future dice roll per success, with the player choosing which rolls are affected before those dice fall. Unused luck rolls expire at the scene's end.

spells.name: Destroy Bindings
spells.action: Instant and contested; successes are compared to the bond's Potency
spells.dice: Resolve + Occult + Fate vs. bond's Potency
spells.description: The mage severs a forced spiritual bond — a soul reattached by Death magic, a spirit imprisoned in a fetish, or a familiar bond — with successes needing to exceed the bond's Potency to take effect. The release is lasting for freed souls and ghosts, though the familiar bond returns to normal at the end of the scene.

spells.name: Gift of Fortune
spells.description: The mage steers destiny so that a specific object finds its way into her possession through entirely plausible circumstances, with delivery time based on physical distance from the caster. The mage needs a Space sympathetic connection to reach objects beyond sensory range. Requires Space 2.

spells.name: Probable Cause
spells.description: The mage ensures near-certainty of success in nearly anything she puts her hand to, gaining the rote quality on one future dice roll per success achieved during casting. This spell cannot be stacked with Exceptional Luck or Superlative Luck — only the most potent takes effect.

spells.name: Sanctify Oaths
spells.description: The mage brings the full metaphysical weight of destiny to bear on oaths sworn by others, binding even unwitting parties into mystical vows that carry the same supernatural consequences as the mage's own personally sworn oath. The participant need not be aware the mage has the means to place metaphysical momentum behind such vows.

spells.name: Sever Oaths
spells.action: Instant and contested; successes are compared to the oath's Potency
spells.dice: Manipulation + Occult + Fate vs. oath's Potency
spells.description: The mage completely severs a Fate-based oath, with successes needing to exceed the oath's Potency to free the target from its compulsion. He may also freely manipulate an oath he personally swore using Fate 2, in which case a single success is sufficient.

spells.name: Break the Chains
spells.action: Instant and contested; successes are compared to the geas' Potency
spells.dice: Wits + Occult + Fate vs. geas Potency
spells.description: The mage dispels or rearranges the conditions of another mage's geas, either altering its specifics or destroying the compulsion entirely; successes must exceed the geas's Potency to take effect. Free Council willworkers prize this rote to prove the limits of forced compliance.

spells.name: Forge Destiny
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Resolve + Gnosis reflexively
spells.dice: Manipulation + Persuasion + Fate vs. Resolve + Gnosis
spells.description: The mage determines the destiny of a living being, adding one die per success to any action that fosters the declared fate and penalizing by one die per success any action that hinders it. Setting Fate's mark on someone can protect the secrecy of the Awakened by drawing people into or away from the occult world.

spells.name: Forge Doom
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Resolve + Gnosis reflexively
spells.dice: Resolve + Intimidation + Fate vs. Resolve + Gnosis
spells.description: The mage declares a living being, place, thing, condition, flaw, or behavior to be someone's doom, increasing all damage dice suffered by the target while acting under the doom's parameters by the spell's successes. Meeting one's doom does not guarantee harm — it only worsens any damage that might already happen.

spells.name: Forge Godsend
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Resolve + Gnosis reflexively
spells.dice: Composure + Survival + Fate vs. Resolve + Gnosis
spells.description: The mage declares a living being, place, thing, condition, or physical strength to be a godsend for someone, subtracting the spell's successes from any damage suffered while the target is possessed of, acting within, or protected by that godsend. In the case of a place, the default area affected is one-yard radius or five cubic yards.

spells.name: Geas
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Resolve + Gnosis reflexively
spells.dice: Presence + Expression + Fate vs. Resolve + Gnosis
spells.description: The mage forcibly compels another person to heed her will as though they had sworn a binding oath, imposing a directive the target must actively work toward or suffer the effects of the Great Curse for the remainder of the geas's Duration. The objective must be possible, though it need not be reasonable.

spells.name: Great Curse
spells.description: A more powerful version of the Evil Eye, the Great Curse blights a target so that every task they undertake surely fails for the duration, affecting all rolls made while the magic is in effect. This spell cannot be combined with the Evil Eye — only the most potent takes effect.

spells.name: Swarm of Locusts
spells.description: The mage creates chaotic Fortean conditions — rains of frogs, every animal in a city block going berserk simultaneously, and similarly improbable occurrences — by surrendering all control of the spell's effects to Fate's hands. Sleepers who witness the chaos are temporarily open to the possibilities of magic and do not act as Paradox witnesses for spells cast in the affected area.

spells.name: Alter Conductivity
spells.description: The mage alters the conductivity of a simple object, rendering an electrical cord completely non-conductive or making concrete conduct electricity like copper; the number of objects affected increases with each success.

spells.name: Craftsman's Eye
spells.description: The mage discovers the proper function of an object with moving parts, potentially aiding Craft Skill rolls at the Storyteller's discretion.

spells.name: Dark Matter
spells.description: The mage gains Mage Sight through the Matter Arcanum, perceiving the resonance of materials and gaining a +1 bonus when studying dense or very dense matter while suffering a −1 penalty for refined or very refined resonance.

spells.name: Detect Substance
spells.description: The mage detects substances in the area around her, capable of detecting concealed weapons by their gunpowder or hidden transmitters by their copper wiring.

spells.name: Discern Composition
spells.description: The mage perceives the fundamental makeup of material structures, determining their composition, weight, and density, and can discern an object's Durability, Size, and Structure.

spells.name: Find the Hidden Hoard
spells.description: The mage finds secret compartments within inanimate material, discovering hidden doors, safes, and vaults; extremely well-designed or precisely engineered compartments may impose a −1 or −2 dice penalty.

spells.name: Alter Accuracy
spells.description: The mage improves the balance and heft of a simple item, granting it the 9-again quality on the next dice roll made using it; each extra success extends this benefit to one additional roll.

spells.name: Eyes of the Earth
spells.description: As the Matter 1 Dark Matter spell, this grants Matter Mage Sight to another mage or supernatural being; if cast upon a Sleeper, it invokes Disbelief immediately even if its Duration is less than one scene.

spells.name: Shape Liquid
spells.description: The mage imparts cohesion to liquid or vapor, forming it into shapes of his choosing or moving it freely; liquid vapor or mist can be affected but purely gaseous substances such as helium or mustard gas cannot.

spells.name: Steel Windows
spells.description: The mage makes an opaque object transparent or vice versa, and can choose to affect only one surface, such as making the outer face of a bank vault door transparent while keeping the inner mechanisms visible.

spells.name: Transmute Water
spells.description: The mage transmutes one common liquid substance into another common liquid substance, such as turning water into milk or orange juice into gasoline; the mage must touch the targeted liquid.

spells.name: Unseen Aegis
spells.description: The mage subtly acts upon inert material in his immediate vicinity to defend himself from harm, as air cushions incoming fists and moisture douses flames; he gains one point of armor per dot he possesses in the Matter Arcanum.

spells.name: Alter Integrity
spells.description: The mage reinforces or degrades an object's Durability by one point per success, with the change also altering the object's Structure accordingly; at this level the mage must touch the targeted object.

spells.name: Armor Piercing
spells.description: The mage bestows the armor-piercing quality upon an object, allowing it to ignore one armor or Durability point on the next roll made using it; each extra success extends this benefit to one additional roll.

spells.name: Jury Rig
spells.description: The mage superimposes pieces of various machines into one another, transposing one quality per success from one mechanical object into another to produce a desired functional combination.

spells.name: Plasticity
spells.description: The mage renders any reasonably homogeneous material malleable for a single turn, making normally inflexible substances such as ice, coal, or lead crystal pliable as very stiff clay and as easy to sculpt by hand.

spells.name: Repair Object
spells.description: The mage repairs an object without leaving seams, restoring one Structure point per success as though the object had never been damaged.

spells.name: Transmute Earth
spells.description: The mage transmutes one common solid substance into another common solid substance, such as turning granite into glass or a car fender into balsa wood; transformations involving precious metals or rare materials are not possible at this level.

spells.name: Verminous Metamorphosis
spells.description: Requiring Matter ••• and Life •••, the mage transforms pieces of lifeless material such as coins or staves into simple vermin — spiders, scorpions, or biting flies — with successes determining the number and size of the resulting swarm.

spells.name: Alter Efficiency
spells.description: The mage improves or degrades a mechanical object's workings, adjusting equipment modifier dice by one per success up to a maximum of plus or minus five, or altering a vehicle's maximum speed or acceleration.

spells.name: Annihilate Matter
spells.description: The mage destroys inert matter by dissolving its atomic lattice, inflicting one point of damage per success without needing to overcome the object's Durability; magical, enhanced, and Artifact items are immune to this spell.

spells.name: Lesser Transmogrification
spells.description: The mage turns a single liquid substance into a single solid substance and vice versa, turning water into stone or wood into ink; this spell cannot transmute objects into impossible combinations such as wood into liquid wood.

spells.name: Reconfigure Object
spells.description: The mage turns an object into a different type of object of comparable complexity, altering its form entirely while its material substance, Durability, Structure, and Size remain unchanged.

spells.name: Shape Air
spells.description: The mage controls the flow and density of air or any gaseous substance within a defined volume, shaping and moving it at up to one yard per Gnosis dot per turn; shaped air has Durability equal to its Strength and can knock down those in its path.

spells.name: Transmute Air
spells.description: The mage transforms one gaseous substance into another gaseous substance, such as turning nerve gas into laughing gas or oxygen into a harmless gas; the spell does not alter the substance's state, only its composition.

spells.name: Transmute Gold
spells.description: The mage transmutes a precious or rare substance such as gold or diamond into a common substance such as wood or mud; unlike Transmute Earth, this spell works on materials of rare or metaphysical significance.

spells.name: Alter Size
spells.description: The mage alters an object's Size, making it larger or smaller by one point of Size per success, within the limits of the caster's Matter Arcanum dots.

spells.name: Annihilate Extraordinary Matter
spells.description: As the Matter 4 Annihilate Matter spell, this destroys enchanted substances normally immune to such attacks, including enhanced or imbued items, Artifacts, and thaumium.

spells.name: Forge Thaumium
spells.description: Requiring Matter ••••• and Prime •••, the mage forges thaumium — a mirrored alloy of perfected gold, silver, and mercury — that when charged with Mana provides a reflexive counterspell against any Arcanum; one success creates a Size 1, Durability 1 piece with capacity for one Mana point.

spells.name: Greater Transmogrification
spells.description: The mage freely interchanges liquid, solid, and gaseous states in any combination, including precious or rare substances such as gold; this expands on Lesser Transmogrification to include all three states and rare materials.

spells.name: Raw Creation
spells.description: The mage creates an object out of nothing up to Size 3, with additional successes allocated to Size or Durability; complex objects such as automobiles may require a reflexive Intelligence + Crafts or Science roll.

spells.name: Self-Repairing Machine
spells.description: The mage gives an object a self-repairing function, with each success enabling the object to automatically recover one Structure point of damage at a rate of one point per 15 minutes.

spells.name: Aura Perception
spells.description: The mage reads a thinking being's aura to discern its mental and emotional state. The spell reveals the aura's color signature but requires the Prime 1 Supernal Vision spell to determine the target's supernatural nature.

spells.name: One Mind, Two Thoughts
spells.description: The mage holds two distinct trains of thought simultaneously, performing two separate extended knowledge tasks at once so long as neither requires intense physical exertion or spellcasting.

spells.name: Sense Consciousness
spells.description: The mage detects the presence of minds within sensory range, from the simplest animal to the most powerful spirit or willworker, and the spell creates a safety zone that no mind can enter without the mage's awareness.

spells.name: Third Eye
spells.description: The mage gains Mage Sight, sensing when nearby beings use exceptional mental powers such as telepathy or ESP, and reading the resonance of a target's will to discern the emotional and psychic qualities behind it.

spells.name: Alter Aura
spells.description: The mage masks his own aura to display any mental or emotional state he chooses, though he cannot alter his nature or appear to be something he is not such as a Sleeper or vampire.

spells.name: Beast Control
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Resolve reflexively
spells.dice: Presence + Animal Ken + Mind vs. Resolve
spells.description: The mage takes telepathic command of a simple-minded animal — a reptile, insect, fish, or invertebrate — compelling it to perform commands that are not completely inimical to its nature.

spells.name: Emotional Urging
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Composure + Gnosis reflexively
spells.dice: Manipulation + Empathy + Mind vs. Composure + Gnosis
spells.description: The mage projects a specific emotion into a target's mind as a powerful suggestion rather than a mandate; the feeling is most convincing when it fits the target's individual situation.

spells.name: First Impressions
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Composure + Gnosis reflexively
spells.description: The mage generates an instant sense of familiarity and trust in a chosen person, adding one die per success to the first Social Attribute-based roll made with that individual.

spells.name: Incognito Presence
spells.description: Witnesses perceive nothing special about the mage and are apt to forget her shortly after she passes; each success adds to the difficulty of noticing or remembering her.

spells.name: Memory Hole
spells.description: The mage partitions parts of his own consciousness so that he does not remember certain thoughts or events, subtracting one die per success from others' rolls to detect lies he tells about those hidden memories.

spells.name: Mental Shield
spells.description: The mage erects a psychic barrier that subtracts one die per Mind dot from spells and powers attempting to mentally control, detect, or influence him, and also lets him see through mental illusions upon first encountering them.

spells.name: Misperception
spells.description: The mage convinces attackers and observers that she is slightly displaced from her actual position, causing blows to go astray and granting armor points equal to her Mind Arcanum dots.

spells.name: Opening the Lidless Eye
spells.description: The mage casts the Third Eye spell upon another mage or supernatural being to read the resonance of their mind and workings; if cast upon a Sleeper, it triggers Disbelief immediately regardless of Duration.

spells.name: Voice from Afar
spells.description: The mage projects one word per success directly into another mind with no supernatural compulsion attached, though an unwilling recipient may reflexively contest to determine whether the voice is genuine.

spells.name: Augment the Mind
spells.description: The mage raises one of his own Mental or Social Attributes by one dot per success, up to a maximum equal to his Mind Arcanum dots; boosting Resolve or Composure also increases Willpower capacity but not supply.

spells.name: Aura Cloak
spells.description: The mage alters another person's aura as he does for himself with Alter Aura, changing only the aura's appearance without affecting the target's actual emotional state.

spells.name: Greater Beast Control
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Resolve reflexively
spells.dice: Presence + Animal Ken + Mind vs. Resolve
spells.description: The mage extends telepathic command to median-intelligence animals — anything up to but not including primates, cetaceans, and cephalopods — compelling them to perform actions not completely inimical to their nature.

spells.name: Imposter
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Composure + Gnosis reflexively
spells.dice: Intelligence + Subterfuge + Mind vs. Composure + Gnosis
spells.description: The mage clouds a target's perceptions to make her believe the caster is someone else — either a real person or a fictional persona — with each success adding a convincing sensory detail to the false image.

spells.name: Mental Wall
spells.description: The mage erects a Mental Shield on another person, protecting a companion's mind against detection and alien influences exactly as if that person had cast the spell themselves.

spells.name: Multi-Tasking
spells.description: The mage can simultaneously perform three mental extended actions or two mental instant actions; none of the parallel tasks can be spellcasting, which demands more than mere mental division.

spells.name: Psychic Assault
spells.description: The mage delivers a crude psychic shock that translates into bashing damage as the target's neurons misfire and muscles spasm; the spell affects vampires despite their undead neurology.

spells.name: Sleep of the Just
spells.description: The mage takes control of her own sleep cycle and dreams, sleeping as much or as little as desired and performing mental extended actions while asleep with the same freedom as while awake.

spells.name: Telepathy
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Resolve + Gnosis reflexively
spells.dice: Wits + Empathy + Mind vs. Resolve + Gnosis
spells.description: The mage opens direct mind-to-mind contact with another sentient being, sharing thoughts, images, and emotions without the constraints of speech; only conscious surface thoughts are accessible at this level.

spells.name: Universal Language
spells.description: The mage translates any idea into or out of any language by reducing it to root concepts, working for spoken words, written words, and encoded signals that exist only as thought.

spells.name: Augment Other Mind
spells.description: As the Mind 3 Augment the Mind spell, this conjunctional spell (also requiring Life ••••) raises one of another human being's Mental or Social Attributes by one dot per success, up to the caster's Mind Arcanum dots.

spells.name: Befuddle
spells.description: The mage degrades one of a target's Mental or Social Attributes by one dot per success, down to a minimum of one; the mage must touch the target.

spells.name: Breach the Vault of Memory
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Resolve + Gnosis reflexively
spells.dice: Manipulation + Subterfuge + Mind vs. Resolve + Gnosis
spells.description: The mage can add, modify, or delete a target's memories in any way he sees fit, with deeper and more fundamental memories requiring greater penalty dice to alter.

spells.name: Dream Traveler
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Composure + Gnosis reflexively
spells.dice: Manipulation + Occult + Mind vs. Composure + Gnosis
spells.description: The mage psychically enters the dreams of a sleeping individual, casting Mind spells within those dreams to reshape the subject's psychic landscape with as much or as little precision as desired.

spells.name: Hallucination
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Resolve + Gnosis reflexively
spells.dice: Intelligence + Subterfuge + Mind vs. Resolve + Gnosis
spells.description: The mage creates a convincing false sensory experience affecting all five senses plus one additional sense per extra success; the illusion may be given a simple programmed action or maintained with concentration each turn.

spells.name: Psychic Projection
spells.description: The mage projects his mind into Twilight as an intangible, semi-transparent form affected only by mind-affecting powers; his body remains alive but comatose during the projection.

spells.name: Psychic Sword
spells.description: The mage performs a greater psychic assault that inflicts lethal damage on a one-for-one basis.

spells.name: Read the Depths
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Composure + Gnosis reflexively
spells.dice: Manipulation + Empathy + Mind vs. Composure + Gnosis
spells.description: The mage telepathically enters a subject's subconscious to read individual memories rather than surface thoughts, and can also divine the target's Virtue and Vice.

spells.name: Supreme Augmentation
spells.description: The mage raises more than one of his Mental and Social Attributes simultaneously, allocating successes freely among them in any combination, with each Attribute's total limited by the mage's Mind dots.

spells.name: Telepathic Control
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Resolve + Gnosis reflexively
spells.dice: Manipulation + Persuasion + Mind vs. Resolve + Gnosis
spells.description: The mage exerts telepathic control over a human or higher mind, compelling actions the target cannot refuse so long as they are not completely suicidal or utterly contrary to the target's deepest nature.

spells.name: Network
spells.description: The mage connects multiple minds simultaneously into a telepathic network, with successes determining how many minds can be linked; targets can share thoughts and coordinate without needing visual or spoken contact.

spells.name: Possession
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Resolve + Gnosis reflexively
spells.dice: Manipulation + Persuasion + Mind vs. Resolve + Gnosis
spells.description: The mage psychically possesses another being, using the host's Physical Attributes and Merits or Flaws tied to their physical or social identity while the host's body lies comatose or watches as an impotent spectator.

spells.name: Psychic Domination
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Resolve + Gnosis reflexively
spells.dice: Presence + Intimidation + Mind vs. Resolve + Gnosis
spells.description: The mage utterly dominates the mind of another thinking being, subjugating even survival instincts and deep moral convictions; unlike Telepathic Control, there is no limit to the thoughts and behaviors that can be forced upon a subject.

spells.name: Psychic Genesis
spells.description: The mage creates a fully self-aware artificial consciousness that can manifest as a Twilight presence or be invested into a physical vessel; the mind dissolves when the spell expires unless designed to accept its own inevitable end.

spells.name: Psychic Reprogramming
spells.action: Extended and contested; target rolls Composure + Gnosis reflexively
spells.dice: Manipulation + Persuasion + Mind vs. Composure + Gnosis
spells.description: The mage performs a deep overhaul of a subject's fundamental psychic makeup, permanently altering or deleting Virtues, Vices, and core personality aspects, though the effect is only prolonged against mages and other supernaturals.

spells.name: Shadow Projection
spells.description: The mage sends his consciousness past the Gauntlet into the Shadow Realm, manifesting as a psychic projection with an ephemeral silver cord to the body and armor equal to his Gnosis and Health.

spells.name: Twilight Temple
spells.description: The mage sculpts Twilight into an immaterial abode accessible only to his psychic form, with Durability equal to his Mind Arcanum and Structure equal to his Gnosis; Spirit 2 wards spirits and Death 2 wards ghosts from the temple.

spells.name: Analyze Enchanted Item
spells.description: The mage scrutinizes an enchanted item to learn its magical properties, resonance, and any attached conditions or curses. With an exceptional success, the mage can determine whether the item is cursed or has other harmful or beneficial circumstances connected to its ownership.

spells.name: Discern Phantasm
spells.description: The mage can see through Prime-based illusions created with manifested Mana, perceiving them for what they truly are; they do not vanish from her sight but are known for what they are. The phantasm's Potency levies dice penalties on the roll.

spells.name: Dispel Magic
spells.action: Instant and contested; successes are compared to the target spell's Potency
spells.dice: Intelligence + Occult + Prime vs. target spell's Potency
spells.description: The mage dismantles or dispels an existing spell, pitting her successes against the target spell's Potency to unweave it. She must have at least one dot in each Arcanum used in the spell being dispelled.

spells.name: Inscribe Grimoire
spells.description: The mage encodes a rote into a magical book readable by other Awakened minds, placing part of her magical knowledge outside herself so others can learn and cast from it. Recording the rote causes the mage to forget how to cast it on her own until she re-learns it.

spells.name: Supernal Vision
spells.description: The mage gains the Mage Sight, activating potent resonance-detecting senses that allow her to perceive magical phenomena, enchanted items, Mana, Hallows, and unseen spells. This sense is best used conjunctionally with other Arcana to analyze their specific magical signatures.

spells.name: Activate Enchanted Item
spells.description: The mage artificially satisfies the trigger condition of a contingent Artifact or imbued item, allowing it to activate on demand within the spell's Duration. This is usually performed after carefully studying the item with Analyze Enchanted Item.

spells.name: Counterspell Prime
spells.action: Instant; successes subtract from the target spell's Potency
spells.description: The mage can counter spells from any Arcanum without needing to identify their components first, and can counter covert spells without first identifying them. This is a broader version of the basic Counterspell effect, covering all Arcana rather than one.

spells.name: Magic Shield
spells.description: The mage creates a shield of Prime energy visible to Mage Sight as ghostly white flames, granting one point of armor per Prime dot against spells that directly target the mage. The shield requires one Mana each time an incoming spell strikes it, and spending one Mana at casting can extend its Duration to one day.

spells.name: Primal Flow
spells.description: The mage casts the Supernal Vision effect upon another mage or supernatural being such as a ghost or werewolf, allowing the target to perceive resonance and magical phenomena for the scene. If cast upon a Sleeper, the spell immediately invokes Disbelief.

spells.name: Squaring the Circle
spells.description: The mage creates a mystical dueling space where two mages can engage in the Duel Arcane, a wizard's war governed by strict rules. The enclosed space enforces the rules of the Duel for its duration.

spells.name: Transform Aura
spells.description: The mage alters the nature of his own aura, changing its apparent quality or hiding it so he appears to be a Sleeper or even an inhuman creature. Only a Supernal Vision spell of higher Potency can detect the true aura beneath the disguise.

spells.name: Unseen Spy
spells.description: The mage hides himself from other willworkers' magical scrutiny, preventing them from sensing when he attempts to scrutinize or dispel their spells. If a spy tries to scrutinize a vigilant caster's spells, the caster makes a reflexive contested roll to detect the intrusion.

spells.name: Armor of the Soul
spells.description: The mage weaves a barrier of Prime energy around his soul, protecting it from being stolen by a spirit or severed by a Death adept. The protection grants one point of armor per Prime dot against magic or powers that specifically affect the soul.

spells.name: Celestial Fire
spells.description: The mage manifests a ball of glowing, celestial fire energy that can be thrown at a target, inflicting one point of bashing damage per success. This celestial fire is not mundane flame — vampires are not especially susceptible to it and it does not induce Rotschreck.

spells.name: Channel Mana
spells.description: The mage channels Mana from a Hallow, tass, or enchanted item into her own Mana pool, drawing one point per success up to her Prime dot maximum. The mage must touch the source.

spells.name: Controlled Dispellation
spells.action: Instant and contested; successes are compared to the target spell's Potency
spells.description: The mage selectively unravels a spell so it only affects those targets he chooses, leaving the spell intact for others. He can effectively make his own Pattern — or the Patterns of those he selects — invisible to the dispelled spell without ending the effect for everyone else.

spells.name: Create Tass
spells.description: The mage solidifies Mana into tass, a physical object of raw crystallized magical energy that can look like anything from a rock to a table. Successes are allocated to the tass's Size and Durability, and at least one Mana must be channeled into it.

spells.name: Disguise Resonance
spells.description: The mage changes the apparent resonance of an area or Hallow, making a powerful site appear to be a dead zone or giving it a false magical signature. This spell cannot be detected except by a Mage Sight spell of higher Potency.

spells.name: Dissolve Tass
spells.description: The mage dissolves tass back into the Tapestry, releasing its bound Mana and Structure back into the environment. Each success dissolves one point of the tass's Mana and one point of its Structure.

spells.name: Ephemeral Enchantment
spells.description: The mage enchants a weapon to strike against Twilight and Shadow realm entities, or an armor to protect against them, for the scene. If the object is a firearm, all ammunition in its magazine, cylinder, and chamber is also enchanted.

spells.name: Imbue Item
spells.description: The mage weaves one or more spells into a physical item so that anyone who wields it gains their benefits, even if they did not cast them. The mage must be capable of casting each spell to be imbued, and each spell costs one Mana per spell in addition to any normal casting cost.

spells.name: Imbue Mana
spells.description: The mage gives a living creature one of his Mana points per success, touching the target during casting. Overloading a Sleeper with more Mana than her Pattern can hold inflicts one Health of bashing damage per excess point as the celestial energy tears her Pattern apart.

spells.name: Ley Lines
spells.description: The mage harnesses a ley line node and taps it as a perpetual source of free energy, determining the form the node takes — such as a pure spring, a tranquil valley, or a mountain peak. The effect lasts as long as nothing disrupts the ley lines by altering the node's architecture or geography.

spells.name: Magic Wall
spells.description: The mage casts a Magic Shield on another person, granting them Prime armor against directly targeted spells. If the target does not have her own Mana pool, the caster must incorporate Mana during casting to allow the shield to hold a number of charges.

spells.name: Phantasm
spells.description: The mage creates an inanimate phantasm woven of Mana threads that appears to Sleepers as a normal object of its type — books, chairs, knives, or stones. The phantasm has one point of Durability, lacks conductivity and magnetism, and cannot fuel a sustained fire.

spells.name: Transform Other Aura
spells.description: The mage alters another person's aura, changing its apparent nature just as Transform Aura does for his own. Cast conjunctionally with Mind 2, the mage can also alter the target's apparent mental and emotional state as seen in the aura.

spells.name: Awaken Hallow
spells.description: The mage awakens a dormant Hallow, restoring it to the level of activity it held before it fell dormant. The target number equals the original level of the Hallow before it became dormant.

spells.name: Eyes of the Awakened
spells.description: The mage bestows temporary Mage Sight upon a Sleeper, allowing her to perceive the Mysteries for the duration without invoking Disbelief. The Sleeper still adversely influences Paradox as a normal witness despite her expanded perception.

spells.name: Marionette
spells.description: The mage creates a potentially animate phantasm of an animal or non-human creature that moves under her direct mental concentration. The phantasm moves as its creature type normally would but collapses into a lifeless heap the moment the mage stops concentrating.

spells.name: Phantasmal Weapon
spells.description: The mage creates a sturdy phantasm capable of functioning as a weapon or armor, with Durability equal to the caster's Prime dots. Each success grants two dots of Physical Attributes allocated to the phantasm as the caster wishes, up to the mage's Prime dot limit in any one trait.

spells.name: Siphon Essence
spells.description: The mage pulls the Essence from a spirit, ghost, or locus and converts it to personal Mana, either adding it to his pool or dispersing it into the Tapestry. Each success siphons one Essence from the targeted entity.

spells.name: Siphon Integrity
spells.description: The mage pulls the Mana out of a mundane lifeless object, inflicting one point of Structure damage per success; this damage is brittle and circumvents the object's Durability. The mage may add the siphoned Mana to his own pool or disperse it into the Tapestry.

spells.name: Supernal Dispellation
spells.action: Instant and contested; successes are compared to the target spell's Potency
spells.dice: Resolve + Occult + Prime vs. target spell's Potency
spells.description: The mage can dispel any spell regardless of which Arcana compose it, with no need to possess dots in those Arcana. His Prime mastery is sufficient alone to unweave any enchantment by attacking its Pattern directly.

spells.name: Suppress Hallow
spells.description: The mage temporarily suppresses an active Hallow, reducing its rating by one dot per success for the duration of one scene. Guardians of the Veil use this to neutralize conspicuous paranormal sources of power before they attract unwanted attention.

spells.name: Create Complex Phantasm
spells.dice: Intelligence + Crafts (for objects) or Medicine (for people) + Prime
spells.description: The mage creates a complex phantasm that can mimic living beings or complex objects such as computers, standing up to mundane scrutiny. The phantasm has the same Mental and Social Attributes as the caster, can speak when compelled, but collapses into a lifeless heap if the mage stops concentrating.

spells.name: Create Hallow
