spells.description: The mage creates a temporary Hallow at a location of his choosing, which immediately begins yielding Mana equal to its rating. The Hallow lasts for up to one month depending on successes, but cannot be made indefinitely permanent.

spells.name: Dead Zone
spells.description: The mage expels all ambient Mana from an area, creating a magic dead zone where spellcasting is penalized and small animals and children may sicken and die. Successes determine the radius or volume of the Dead Zone, and a mage within it cannot use Channel Mana to draw from another source.

spells.name: Forge Tulpa
spells.description: The mage creates a sentient being of pure Mana, investing it with Physical, Mental, and Social Attributes allocated to the mage's Prime dots; requires Mind 5 as a conjunctional Arcanum. The tulpa may be free-willed or bound to the mage's will, but cannot be given an indefinite Duration and dissolves when the spell expires.

spells.name: Siphon Mana
spells.description: The mage siphons Mana at sensory range from an unwilling mage's pool and adds it to his own, channeling one point per success. Sleepers and mundane creatures cannot be siphoned as they lack spendable Mana; each successive casting against the same target in a scene suffers a cumulative –2 dice penalty.

spells.name: Correspondence
spells.description: The mage analyzes the sympathetic connections between things, people, and places, determining the degree of their bond and whether any connections have been concealed or tampered with.

spells.name: Finder
spells.description: The mage tracks a specific chosen object—such as a weapon, idol, or ballpoint pen—knowing its location at all times within sensory range.

spells.name: Omnivision
spells.description: The mage perceives 360 degrees around himself simultaneously, seeing in all directions at once and making it nearly impossible to be surprised from behind or ambushed by anything he can see.

spells.name: Spatial Awareness
spells.description: The mage gains Mage Sight attuned to Space, allowing him to perceive spatial distortions, teleportation origin points, Wards, and other manipulations of the spatial fabric in the local area.

spells.name: Spatial Map
spells.description: The mage creates a constant mental spatial map of her surroundings, with each success eliminating one penalty die from ranged attack rolls and negating penalties from opponents’ concealment or spatially based conditions.

spells.name: Apportation
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Composure reflexively
spells.description: With the aid of another Arcanum such as Forces or Matter, the mage draws an item through a scrying window to her current location or sends something to a remotely viewed location, contested if the object is held or attended.

spells.name: Bestow Spatial Awareness
spells.description: As the Space 1 Spatial Awareness spell, except the mage casts it upon another mage or supernatural being such as a ghost or werewolf; if cast upon a Sleeper it immediately invokes Disbelief.

spells.name: Conceal Sympathy
spells.description: The mage decreases the apparent sympathetic connection between himself and any person, place, or thing, with three successes reducing an Intimate connection to appear merely Encountered to outside observers using sympathetic magic.

spells.name: Follow Through
spells.description: The mage can step through a nearby spatial doorway such as a Portal and arrive at its original terminus destination, forced to go exactly where the previous spatial door led with no ability to redirect.

spells.name: Scrying
spells.description: The mage remotely views a location and can employ all her senses through this perceptual window to see, hear, smell, and even touch things on the far side, modified by the strength of her sympathetic connection to the location.

spells.name: Untouchable
spells.description: The mage performs subtle intuitive tweaks to the local fabric of space, slipping from hostile attention and gaining one point of armor per Space dot; spending one Mana extends the duration to one day.

spells.name: Ward
spells.description: The mage creates a spatial barrier that locks a locale’s Space template in place, barring the passage of other Space spells including remote sensing, teleportation, and spatial tampering; the Ward can be reinforced with successive castings.

spells.name: Avoidance Tactics
spells.description: The mage casts the Untouchable spell on others, granting the target one point of armor per Space dot; an optional Mana expenditure extends the duration to one day.

spells.name: Ban
spells.description: The mage adds a Ban to an existing Ward, specifying a phenomenon governed by another Arcanum—such as insects, metal objects, or spirits—that cannot enter the Warded area by any means whatsoever.

spells.name: Destroy the Threads
spells.description: The mage destroys a sympathetic connection, reducing the target’s link to a specific person, place, or thing by one degree per success on the standard connection chart.

spells.name: Multispatial Perception
spells.description: The mage scrys multiple locations simultaneously at one location per success, catching only a glimpse at each but able to notice obvious strange occurrences or anything blatantly out of place.

spells.name: New Threads
spells.description: The mage creates a sympathetic connection by fortifying the elusive bond between things, adding one degree of sympathetic connection per success between a target and any person, place, or thing of his choosing.

spells.name: Portal
spells.action: Extended (target number = degree of sympathetic connection)
spells.description: The mage creates a spatial warp through which the caster and anyone else can step from one point in Creation to another without traversing the intervening space; size and destination depend on successes and the mage’s sympathetic connection.

spells.name: Ranged Blow
spells.description: The mage can punch, kick, or stab a target from afar by transferring force through empty space, making Brawl or Weaponry attacks against targets within sensory range for one Mana per attack.

spells.name: Co-Location
spells.description: The mage exists simultaneously in one location plus one additional location per success, reflexively choosing each turn which location he physically inhabits through instantaneous spatial transference.

spells.name: Pocket Realm
spells.description: The mage creates a private pocket universe of Size 20 into which he can escape with his familiar and possessions; the space is featureless and gray without other Arcana to furnish it.

spells.name: Portal Key
spells.description: The mage attunes a portal he has created to a specific activation trigger—a word, gesture, or inherent quality such as a family member’s presence—controlling exactly who can and cannot open or pass through it.

spells.name: Safe Keeping
spells.description: The mage places an item into his Pocket Realm for safekeeping, where it remains until retrieved; if the Pocket Realm spell expires, items reappear exactly where they were placed into the realm.

spells.name: Suspension
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Composure + Gnosis reflexively
spells.description: The mage entangles a being or object in invisible spatial strands that loop space back on itself, boxing the subject in and rendering it incapable of leaving a defined volume of roughly five cubic yards.

spells.name: Teleportation
spells.description: The mage traverses space without physical movement, instantly arriving at any destination to which he has a sympathetic connection, with spellcasting penalties based on the weakness of that connection.

spells.name: Dimensional Axis
spells.action: Extended (target number = one success per location)
spells.description: The mage causes multiple distinct locations to exist simultaneously in the same space, stacking them so that objects from each location coexist; navigating or interacting with such layered space requires a reflexive Intelligence + Investigation roll.

spells.name: Hide Space
spells.description: The mage creates a hidden sanctum that exists only partway inside the conventional concept of a place, accessible only through convoluted and very specific spatial paths unknown to anyone not shown the way.

spells.name: Labyrinth
spells.description: The mage mutates the dimensional axes of an area so that every path of egress leads somewhere unexpected, trapping intruders in a spatially folded maze from which escape requires navigating back to square one or finding the mage’s hidden exit.

spells.name: Manifold Presence
spells.description: Requiring Space ••••• and Life •••• with optional Matter ••••• and Mind •• or •••, the mage exists simultaneously in multiple physical locations at once with each copy being a genuine extension of his body capable of independent action, sensation, and suffering harm.

spells.name: Oubliette
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Composure + Gnosis reflexively
spells.description: Requiring Space ••••• plus Forces •••, Mind ••••, and Time •••••, this conjunctional spell banishes a victim into a pocket of looped space where she sees herself reflected across gulfs of space and time, losing Willpower from relentless visions until she returns broken.

spells.name: Shrink/Expand
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Stamina + Gnosis reflexively
spells.dice: Intelligence + Medicine (for living creatures) or Crafts or Science (for objects) + Space
spells.description: Requiring Space ••••• and Life •• (base life forms), Life ••• (median life forms), Life •••• (human beings), or Matter ••, the mage warps the spatial axes of a target to shrink or grow its Size by one point per success, from a minimum of Size 1 to a maximum equal to the mage’s Gnosis.

spells.name: Worlds Collide
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Stamina + Gnosis reflexively
spells.dice: Resolve + Investigation + Space vs. Stamina + Gnosis
spells.description: The mage superimposes the spatial axes of multiple people or objects, forcing them to coexist in the same location and inflicting one point of aggravated damage per success as their molecular lattices collide and drive each other apart.

spells.name: Coaxing the Spirit
spells.description: The mage whispers to a spirit sleeping within a material object and coaxes it to aid him, making the object easier to use for the scene. Each success provides a +1 dice bonus on a single roll made within the spell's Duration.

spells.name: Exorcist's Eye
spells.description: The mage detects the presence of a possessing entity within a terrestrial being, sensing whether a spirit, ghost, or other entity rides a host. Knowledgeable mages may identify the type of spirit inhabiting the individual, though that information is not necessarily conveyed by this spell.

spells.name: Second Sight
spells.description: The mage gains the Mage Sight and the ability to perceive spiritual phenomena including spirit Numina, werewolf Gifts, and loci. He can also determine the Strength of the local Gauntlet.

spells.name: Spirit Tongue
spells.description: The mage can see, hear, and speak with spirits and detect their unseen presence even if they are hiding or have chosen not to reveal themselves. Without a means of crossing the Gauntlet, he can converse only with Twilight spirits.

spells.name: Ephemeral Shield
spells.description: The mage wraps himself in ephemeral armor that defends against physical attacks and attacks made by ephemeral entities, gaining one point of armor per dot in the Spirit Arcanum. By spending one Mana the Duration can last for one day.

spells.name: Gossamer Touch
spells.description: The mage gains the power to physically interact with spirits and spiritual things in the Twilight state, touching spirits and being touched in return. She cannot affect ghosts or mental projections without additional Arcana.

spells.name: Granting Second Sight
spells.description: The mage casts the Spirit 1 "Second Sight" spell upon another mage or supernatural being such as a ghost or werewolf, granting them Spirit Arcanum perceptions. If cast on a Sleeper, it invokes Disbelief immediately even if its Duration is less than one scene.

spells.name: Lesser Spirit Summons
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Resistance reflexively
spells.description: The mage calls out to summon a specific spirit or sends a general summons to the nearest one within sensory range, compelling it to come to him. The summoned spirit must remain near the caster for the spell's Duration unless the caster allows it to leave.

spells.name: Peer Across the Gauntlet
spells.description: The mage can peer across the Gauntlet into the Shadow Realm, glimpsing the spiritual reflection of the material world and taking an instant action to concentrate and glimpse past the Gauntlet. If in the Shadow Realm, he can use this spell to peer into the material realm instead.

spells.name: Place of Power
spells.description: The mage can fortify or weaken the Gauntlet at a Hallow, modifying its Strength by one point per success in either direction up to a limit equal to the Hallow's rating. The Gauntlet's Strength cannot be reduced to zero at this level.

spells.name: Soul Jar
spells.description: As the Death 2 "Soul Jar" spell, this enchants a sealable container to trap a spirit within it, preventing the spirit from departing on its own until released.

spells.name: Control Spirit
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Resistance reflexively
spells.description: The mage can force a spirit to perform an action, issuing a single simple command per success. Complex commands require multiple successes.

spells.name: Exorcism
spells.action: Extended (target number = spirit's Willpower) and contested; target rolls Power + Resistance reflexively
spells.description: The mage forcibly expels a possessing spirit or haunting ghost from the body it inhabits or frees it from its physical fetter. The ghost or spirit contests the exorcism by rolling Power + Resistance.

spells.name: Familiar Pact
spells.description: The mage forges a personal pact with a spirit familiar, creating a lasting bond between willworker and spirit. Only one familiar may be pacted to the same person at a time and the bond cannot be dispelled.

spells.name: Greater Spirit Summons
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Resistance reflexively
spells.description: The mage can issue a summons to a Shadow Realm spirit of whatever power, known or unknown, compelling it to come to him. Spirits forcibly summoned are almost always resentful and may be enraged.

spells.name: Harm Spirit
spells.description: The mage can damage a spirit's Corpus, inflicting one point of lethal damage per success on a manifested or Twilight spirit.

spells.name: Numinous Shield
spells.description: The mage creates a personal ward against spirit powers and Numina, gaining one point of armor per dot in the Spirit Arcanum that works only against spirit powers such as Numina and werewolf Gifts. By spending one Mana, the Duration can last for one day.

spells.name: Reaching
spells.description: The mage can physically reach through the Gauntlet in a limited fashion, touching and being touched by beings and objects in the Shadow Realm. This also allows the mage to interact with the spirit realm when he cannot or will not cross the Gauntlet.

spells.name: Restore Corpus
spells.description: The mage can heal a spirit's Corpus, restoring one point of damage per success.

spells.name: Restore Lost Soul
spells.action: Extended (target number = weeks the soul has been lost)
spells.description: The mage can restore a severed or stolen soul to its original owner, with the target number equal to one success per week the soul has been gone. If the soul has been attached to a new body, the mage must first use Fate 4 "Destroy Bindings" to detach it.

spells.name: Rouse Spirit
spells.description: The willworker awakens the dormant spirit slumbering within a material object, giving the roused spirit a measure of sentience that increases with time, use, and care. An awakened object begins as a Rank 1 spirit and can grow more powerful over time.

spells.name: Spirit Road
spells.description: The mage opens a road across the Gauntlet, a lane where he and others can cross over to the Shadow Realm and where spirits from the Shadow Realm can cross into the material world. The road remains for one hour and the mage cannot stop others from using it.

spells.name: Bind Spirit
spells.action: Extended (target number = spirit's Rank) and contested; target rolls Resistance reflexively
spells.description: The mage can bind a spirit into the material world, tying the creature to a given location which may be a form of imprisonment or even torture. When undertaken with indefinite Duration, this spell can confine a being to a place forever.

spells.name: Create Fetish
spells.action: Extended (target number = spirit's Rank) and contested; target rolls Resistance reflexively
spells.description: The mage invites or compels a spirit to inhabit an object and lend its powers to that object in the form of a fetish, with the spirit's target number equal to its Rank. The spirit releases its Numina only upon the fetish's activation.

spells.name: Essence Gift
spells.description: The mage converts Mana into Essence and channels it into a spirit, with each success allowing him to convert one Mana into one Essence. This conjunctional spell requires Spirit 4 and Prime 4.

spells.name: Grant Familiar
spells.description: The mage can forge a familiar pact for another mage as the Spirit 3 "Familiar Pact" spell, using the advanced prolonged Duration factors. Both the mage and the spirit must be willing participants.

spells.name: Medicine Bag
spells.description: The mage creates an object that can store Essence, with the target number equal to one success per point of Essence the bag can hold up to Gnosis + Spirit. Any mage can use the Medicine Bag by casting "Sacramental Chain" to store Essence or take it out.

spells.name: Road Master
spells.description: The mage can control access to the Spirit Roads he creates, deciding who may use them and approving who can walk his roads. He can also step sideways across the Gauntlet without creating a Spirit Road by spending one Mana.

spells.name: Sacramental Chain
spells.description: The mage can channel Essence from a spirit or locus and give it to another spirit or place it into something that can hold it, with one Essence point channeled per success. With Prime 4, the caster can also convert the Essence into Mana.

spells.name: Spirit Guardian
spells.action: Extended (target number = spirit's Rank) and contested; target rolls Resistance reflexively
spells.description: The mage binds a spirit guardian to a person, object, or place to watch over its charge to the best of its ability. While weak spirits are easy to bind, strong spirits are difficult to fetter but have many capabilities to bring to bear.

spells.name: Spirit Possession
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Resistance reflexively
spells.description: The mage can cause a spirit to possess a living creature's body, with the targeted spirit gaining the Possession Numina if it does not normally possess that power. A possessing spirit retains all its own powers but none of the host's paranormal capabilities.

spells.name: Control Gauntlet
spells.description: The mage can increase or decrease the Gauntlet's Strength as the Spirit 2 "Place of Power" spell but without requiring a Hallow, potentially making the local Gauntlet nonexistent or nigh impermeable.

spells.name: Materialize Spirit
spells.action: Instant and contested; target rolls Resistance reflexively
spells.description: The mage can cause a spirit (including a ghost) to materialize and take on a manifest form in the material world; a spirit that cannot normally incarnate may be given physical substance this way. The manifestation takes whatever form the spirit naturally has, though it appears slightly transparent.

spells.name: Shadow Slave
spells.description: The mage infuses conjured shadows (using the Death Arcanum) with an animating spirit, creating a bound creation fettered to the caster's will. This conjunctional spell requires Spirit 5 and Death 3.

spells.name: Shape Spirit
spells.action: Extended and contested; target rolls Resistance reflexively
spells.description: The mage can create a spirit from free-floating ephemera or alter an existing spirit so fundamentally that it becomes a new entity with different appearance, mindset, traits, and powers. When creating a spirit, successes buy dots of Rank, Attributes, Influence, and Numina on a one-for-one basis.

spells.name: Spirit Court
spells.description: The mage establishes a spirit court of her own in which she is the ranking "spirit noble," attracting courtiers, enforcers, spies, and other spirit hangers-on of no higher Rank than the lesser of her Spirit Arcanum or Gnosis. Any spiritual alliances the mage makes are rendered null when the spell expires.

spells.name: Spirit Manse
spells.description: The mage creates a sanctum in the Shadow Realm, with its volume and features determined by the number of successes using the Area-Affecting chart. Ordinary features require no successes to create while exceptional features require one or more successes.
